title: Endogenous calmodulin co-purified with VPS13s detected by western blotting
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5e5va","text":"VPS13C is a ER-lysosome contact site protein that is thought to transport lipids between the two organellar membranes and important for lysosomal membrane damage repair. We employed structure-function analysis of purified VPS13C and uncovered novel regulatory mechanisms. This protocol describes how we see calmodulin consistently bind to VPS13C and VPS13 from Chaetomium thermophilum by western blotting. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":361,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2gke3","text":"anti-calmodulin antibody (Cat. #HY-P82082, MCE)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"abehv","text":"goat anti-rabbit HRP conjugate (Cat. #AP307P, Sigma Aldrich)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fdoqp","text":"ECL substrates (Cat. #32106, Thermo Scientific)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"66nar","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Following the expression and purification protocol (doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rm7vz92d4gx1/v1), either C-terminally tagged VPS13C or N-terminally tagged Ct VPS13 was expressed in Expi293F cells.

For better visualization of calmodulin, resolve the eluted proteins on 4–20% Tris-glycine precast gels. For better visualization of VPS13s, resolve the eluted proteins on 3-8% Tris-acetate precast gels.
Proteins were purified in buffer B (200 mM NaCl, 50 mM HEPES, pH 7.2, 6% glycerol, 1 mM TCEP) supplemented with either Ca 2+ or EGTA.
Wash membranes three times with TBST, each for 00:15:00 .

Incubate membranes for 01:00:00 at Room temperature with goat anti-rabbit HRP-conjugated secondary antibody Goat Anti-Rabbit IgG Antibody, (H+L) HRP conjugate MilliporeSigma Catalog #AP307P diluted 1:1000 in TBST.
Wash membranes three times with TBST, each for 00:15:00 .
Incubate membranes Overnight at 4 °C with anti-calmodulin primary antibody Calmodulin MedChemExpress Catalog #HY-P82082 diluted 1:1000 in 3% BSA/TBST.
Block membranes for 01:00:00 at Room temperature in 3% BSA in TBST.
Transfer proteins to PVDF membranes at 90 V for 01:30:00 at 4 °C in transfer buffer (25 mM Tris, 192 mM glycine, 20% methanol).
Develop membranes with ECL substrate and image using an ImageQuant LAS 4000.
creator: Dazhi Li
